title: Replace Step Counter with Manual Entry + AI Calorie Estimator

requirements.text: Replace the automatic step counter widget in the Fitness section with a manual step entry input field. The user should be able to type in a number of steps directly. Remove or hide the DeviceMotion-based StepTrackerWidget and useStepTracker hook usage from the UI.

quotes: Just make it to enter the steps manual

acceptanceCriteria: A numeric input field labeled 'Steps' (or similar) is visible in the Fitness section where the step tracker widget previously appeared.
The user can type any non-negative integer into the field.
The automatic step counter widget (StepTrackerWidget) is no longer rendered.
No DeviceMotion permission prompt is triggered on page load or interaction.

requirements.text: When the user enters a step count manually, display an AI-calculated estimate of calories burned based on those steps. Use a standard heuristic (e.g., approximately 0.04–0.05 kcal per step, adjusted by the user's body weight from their fitness profile if available) to compute the estimate. Show the result inline near the step input, updating in real time as the user types.

quotes: ai gives no. Of calories burnt with those

acceptanceCriteria: A calorie burn estimate is displayed next to or below the step input field (e.g., 'Estimated calories burned: 210 kcal').
The estimate updates dynamically as the user changes the step value.
If the user's body weight is available from their fitness profile, it is factored into the calculation.
If no profile weight is available, a reasonable default weight (e.g., 70 kg) is used as a fallback.
The calorie output is labeled clearly and attributed to the step count entered.

requirements.text: Persist the manually entered step count to the backend daily metrics (the existing logDailyMetrics mutation) when the user saves or submits the daily metrics form, replacing the auto-filled step value that previously came from the step tracker hook.
acceptanceCriteria: The DailyMetricsForm (or equivalent) uses the manually entered step value instead of the auto-filled value from useStepTracker.
Submitting the form saves the step count to the backend correctly.
Previously saved step counts load back into the input field when the form is rendered with existing data.

constraints: Do not modify any file under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
Do not change the backend data schema for DailyMetrics — only update how the frontend populates the steps field.
Calorie calculation from steps must be client-side only; do not add a new backend endpoint for it.
nonGoals: Fixing or re-enabling the DeviceMotion/accelerometer-based automatic step counter.
Any changes to the Finance section.
Adding a new backend actor method solely for calorie-from-steps calculation.
